entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 30 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 30 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-30 04:14AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • AFL 2022 round 20 LIVE updates: Port power clear of Pies, GWS lose Coniglio in Sydney derby
  • Birmingham 2022: All the action from Day 1 of the Commonwealth Games
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • Underrated and overlooked: Seven of Melbourne’s unsung suburbs
  • US House passes assault weapons ban
  • COVID-19 ‘challenges’ Victoria as another 61 deaths reported
  • ‘We are seeking a momentous change’: Albanese reveals Voice referendum question
  • NRL claims high tackle crackdown has slashed concussions by 43 per cent
  • Millions of Britons farewell their beloved Neighbours
  • ‘I am from Tuvalu and my country is going to sink’: Comm Games team you should be backing
  • High stakes as WA Liberals weigh reform
  • Dellavedova earns return to NBA with Sacramento Kings as United look to plan B
  • ‘I left my $200k a year job to become a cleaner’: Why people quit office work
  • Boxall a quiet observer as his stars Titmus, O’Callaghan fight out 200m final
  • Accused arsonist in France a fireman seeking ‘social recognition’
  • Land-lease community viable alternative when downsizing
  • ‘Dumb moment’: Cleary apologises as he faces six-week ban for dangerous throw
  • Australia blitz the boards with golden start at the velodrome
  • First monkeypox deaths recorded outside Africa in Brazil and Spain
  • Chalmers sets large agenda as economic clouds darken
  • Chalmers, the kind of treasurer the Liberals longed for
  • A broken Voice is no option: Australia’s moment to unite or fail
  • Swearing allegiance to the Queen ‘ridiculous’: Republic minister
  • Anthony Albanese’s speech at Garma Festival annotated
  • The latest illustrations from artist Simon Letch
  • The latest illustrations from artist Andrew Dyson
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Davidson

  • Best of cartoons, July 29, 2022

  • Indigenous Voice deserves bipartisan support
  • Train your own nurses, Australia told amid global shortage
  • Labor’s climate target will help drive businesses’ green power shift: Rio Tinto
  • Falling enrolments, high fees threaten viability of Sydney’s Jewish schools
  • Voice to parliament a chance to heal hurts and grow together
  • Irrigators ‘gifted’ water licences by NSW ministers, communities claim
  • Modern cities like Sydney becoming ‘ghettos of rich people’, architects warn
  • Russia, Ukraine trade blame after strikes kill dozens of Ukrainian POWs
  • Death toll climbs as Kentucky floods expected to worsen
  • It’s Coleen Rooney!: Rebekah Vardy loses ‘Wagatha Christie’ stoush
  • ‘Which polling is that?’: Jacinda Ardern confident despite bumps
  • ‘Not colony nor province’: Zelensky marks statehood day as Russian forces attack Kyiv outskirts
  • Another Vogue cover, another controversy, this time in Ukraine
  • Brazil grants permit to pave highway through heart of Amazon forest
  • Firearms, $4.5 million worth of drugs seized in 11-day police operation
  • Dead koala, border collie among animals dumped at inner-city park
  • Ben Roberts-Smith’s fate in the hands of one man
  • The Torres Strait Islander elders lawyering up to stop their homes from sinking
  • ‘It was guerrilla warfare’: Inside the fight for the future of the Australian Club
  • Finding a nation’s Voice: The pot-holed road to referendum and reform
  • A tale of two treaties
  • Should we be worried about the spread of monkeypox?
  • Why the Coalition risks a backlash if it breaks the Voice
  • COVID, contracts and bitter feuds: Why a Kilmore school collapsed
  • Good Weekend Superquiz and Saturday Target Time, July 30
  • Inflation rates are high - this is where Sydneysiders will see the biggest price increases
  • We’ve become a nation of shopaholics, but can it last?
  • We’re struggling with inflation because we misread the pandemic
  • Game, set, match Cox Architecture for Rosewall Arena facelift
  • Listed property giants face acid test in reporting season
  • Hawthorn Woolworths deal shows supermarkets still market darlings
  • Neighbourhood malls in hot demand with $850m in listings
  • Outspoken billionaire Jack Ma escapes Beijing’s crosshairs by giving up his power
  • Origin Energy locks in coal to ease power crunch
  • Never mind home alone, interns arrive to empty offices
  • Let’s discuss turnover contagion, shift shock and great regret
  • The show must go on ... but what if it doesn’t?
  • Survey: print media portrayal of teachers doesn’t help recruitment
  • As good as it gets - don’t miss this five-star stunner
  • Virtuoso with a story to tell makes ‘new’ opera house sing
  • Tyler, the Creator exudes mix of danger and excitement in Sydney show
  • If you had a disease that could kill you, would you want to know?
  • Anh Do shares his vision for a Marvel-style universe full of his characters
  • Real-life drama behind the scenes of Aquamamma
  • ‘I’m deeply remorseful’: Will Smith apologises to Chris Rock over Oscars slap
  • Australia has a special role to play in fighting the death penalty
  • Miles Franklin winner among contenders for The Age book of the year award
  • Have online sleuths put Poirot on the scrapheap?
  • What to read next: Life-affirming lessons and stylish fashion advice
  • A migrant’s struggle to conjure a normal life into existence
  • Whatever happened to the giants of poetry, and where are they today?

  • There’s a body, a murderer and a whole lot more to think about

  • The best things to see and do in Sydney in August
  • ‘We’re all guilty of using technology as the digital babysitter’
  • How bad is Twitter’s bot problem?
  • Big tech’s winners and losers as ad market slumps and recession bites

Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Washington Post: 30 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

  • The most Midwestern things on Earth, according to data

  • Secret Service’s ‘ludicrous’ deletion of Jan. 6 phone data baffles experts
  • Slowly but surely, robots will wind up in our clothes

  • Tilden Gardens co-op apartment offered at $2.6 million
  • Chips Act Skips US Leaders for Asian Winners

  • European Banks Blow the Roof Off, For Now

  • What to Know About Bolsonaro-Lula Showdown in Brazil
  • What’s Driving the US-China Spat Over Audits and Delisting
  • What’s Next for China Evergrande, Crushed by Debt?

  • America, where we demonize our enemies and demand purity from friends
  • Let’s say Biden isn’t the nominee. Here’s who runs — and wins.
  • Joe Manchin shocks Republicans by revealing he is a Democrat
  • Why Clarence Thomas shouldn’t have backed down
  • Enough with the ‘is this a recession?’ blather
  • Ron DeSantis is taking on woke corporations. Good.
  • Marvel comics are now Penguin Classics. Time to turn classics into Marvel.
  • Tony Dow as Wally Cleaver was a Grade A big brother
  • 2024? Biden should stay focused on the legacy he’s creating now.
  • Biden’s working through covid is bad for America’s public health
  • Blaming social media for academia’s ruin misses a larger, darker truth
  • Assault weapons are deadly. But manufacturers care only about profits.
  • A new chief at Metro, facing a financial cliff

  • Trans people shouldn’t have to hide to help Democrats win
  • Brittney Griner’s detention should prompt fixes to U.S. drug laws

  • Cartoons by Drew Sheneman, Pedro X. Molina, Matt Davies and Tim Campbell

  • We don’t want any missed flags in Virginia
  • The devastating Chesapeake blue crab collapse
  • Mr. Biden can do the right thing — by vowing not to run

Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 30 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Delivering votes lures politicians to Unification Church orbit
  • Bribery scandal latest: Olympic exec pushed for faster approvals
  • KDDI says it will compensate 36 million users for massive outage
  • LDP leaders: ‘no comment’ on ties to Unification Church
  • BA.5 Omicron subvariant seen leading charge in the 7th wave
  • Publishers sue ex-Mangamura operator for 1.9 billion yen
  • Technical intern program will be overhauled, says justice minister
  • Students lose out on usual science experiments during pandemic
  • JR East reveals a third of its rail lines are bleeding money
  • Workers at child care centers seek to embed gender equality early on
  • Japan’s Hayashi says ‘logic of brute force’ gaining traction in Indo-Pacific
  • U.S., Japan to cooperate on semiconductors as part of new economic dialogue
  • Disneyland eyes more complex ticketing to level visitor numbers
  • China's Xi warns Biden over Taiwan, calls for cooperation
  • For Taiwan, Pelosi visit is about U.S., China controlling risk
  • China accuses U.S. of ‘navigation bullying’ in South China Sea
  • Biden, Xi could meet in person, US official says
  • N. Korea claims no new fever cases amid doubts over COVID-19 data
  • Stargazers say traces of June 29 fireball likely fell across Tokyo
  • Calls to level the playing field in the Japanese film industry
  • EDITORIAL: Poor test scores show more policy efforts needed to benefit students
  • VOX POPULI: Was gift to Japan from ancient China a rush job after all?
  • VOX POPULI: Mutability and invariability lie in 150 years of Japan’s railways
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 30 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 30 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-30 03:15AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong’s retail landscape is changing as landlords lure restaurants to fill spaces abandoned by shops during pandemic
  • Old Hong Kong bakeries are dying, go while you still can – buy an egg tart or pineapple bun before they all disappear
  • What next for Mirror? Before tragedy struck, Hong Kong boy band’s soaring success was a bright spot amid pandemic gloom
  • Hong Kong’s Fire Services Department reports rise in heatstroke cases as residents venture outside amid scorching temperatures
  • Evergrande dangles sweeteners for time from bondholders and creditors to deliver a workout plan for the world’s largest real estate debt woes
  • ‘I feel unsettled’: counselling hotlines flooded with calls after accident at Mirror concert in Hong Kong
  • Hong Kong engineers say human error played part in Mirror concert tragedy, as officials place blame on show organiser
  • Hong Kong youngsters should prepare to be ambassadors to the world, Chief Executive John Lee says
  • InnoStars Awards recognise 9 for innovative inventions and work, including Hong Kong scientist who developed Covid-19 test in 3 weeks
  • Who are Mirror and how do they compare with BTS and Hong Kong’s ‘Four Heavenly Kings’ in their prime?
  • Coronavirus Hong Kong: high-risk patients aged under 60 eligible to receive Covid-19 antivirals as authorities push to reduce severe cases
  • Hong Kong national security law: 4 ex-members of defunct student activist group face up to 10 years in jail after admitting advocating for city’s ‘liberation’
  • Hair trigger: why Hong Kong schools are against schoolboys having long hair
  • ‘Abandon arrogance, face fact that national security law has restored stability in Hong Kong’: Beijing to UN Human Rights Committee on report proposing repeal of legislation
  • How much does it really cost to raise a child in Hong Kong?
  • Standard Chartered second-quarter profit beats estimates as several Asian markets rebound ‘well from the Covid-19 pandemic’
  • Accident at Mirror concert will be fully investigated, Hong Kong leader vows, as surgeons fight to help injured dancer walk again
  • China’s bicycle kingdom returns as fuel price, coronavirus bring two-wheelers back in vogue
  • Chinese scientists build bird-zapping laser to guard airports
  • China’s pledge to give green lights to more technology deals points to an easing of restrictions on Big Tech, analysts say
  • China’s military steps up development of ship-based warplanes to keep up with aircraft carrier advances
  • Prospect of a Nancy Pelosi trip to Taiwan jolts Washington as well as Beijing
  • US and Japan launch economic dialogue aimed at pushing back against China
  • No sign of Chinese build-up ahead of Nancy Pelosi’s potential Taiwan trip, White House says
  • Russia’s ‘traditional friendship’ with China remains strong, foreign minister Sergey Lavrov says
  • Alibaba to scale up cross-border e-commerce activities, engage more local exporters in joint campaign with hometown Hangzhou
  • Nancy Pelosi set for Asia trip after Chinese leader Xi Jinping warns US not to ‘play with fire’ over Taiwan
  • Tech war: US chips act casts shadow over Beijing’s semiconductor self-sufficiency drive
  • China launches discipline probe into 3 more Henan officials as fallout from banking scam continues
  • Another heavyweight involved in China’s chip independence drive comes under official investigation: Caixin
  • Pelosi Taiwan visit: region ‘tense’ as Chinese navy watches US warships, holds drills in South China Sea
  • CanSino’s inhaled booster vaccine stronger against Omicron BA.1 subvariant than Sinovac
  • China’s new railway tunnel squeezed by tectonic crush in Tibetan snow mountain
  • Chinese, Indian foreign ministers at Shanghai bloc summit as PLA ship heads to Sri Lanka
  • Delivery firm Missfresh collapses as another Nasdaq-listed Chinese firm falls prey to weakening economy
  • China’s Politburo to ensure delivery of unfinished homes to prevent mortgage-boycott crisis from escalating
  • Former Chinese police boss Fu Zhenghua admits corruption
  • From Singapore to Hong Kong and Japan, is a 4-day work week a pipe dream for Asia?
  • Coronavirus: North Korea claims no new fever cases amid doubts over Covid data
  • World Bank refuses new funding for bankrupt Sri Lanka
  • Can Philippines overcome crisis of investor confidence amid inflation, corruption, debt woes?
  • US scrutiny of solar products from China’s Xinjiang could ‘create opportunities’ for Indian manufacturers
  • Glass half empty: Why Japan’s love affair with Beaujolais Nouveau has soured
  • Philippines detects first case of monkeypox
  • Singapore, New York see highest jump in rental prices this year, while Hong Kong sees decline
  • US, Japan discuss supply chains, semiconductors in ‘2-plus-2’ talks in face of China’s rise and Russia’s war
  • Ukraine war: Zelensky says prison attack that killed dozens ‘a war crime’, while Russia claims Kyiv carried out strike
  • US House passes assault rifle ban, but bill likely doomed to fail in Senate
  • Pope visits remote territory on the edge of the Arctic for final apology of his Canadian tour
  • Elon Musk files countersuit under seal vs Twitter over US$44 billion deal
  • Russian operative charged with using US groups to spread propaganda
  • Spain and Brazil report first monkeypox-related deaths outside Africa
  • At least 53 killed and 16 missing in Iran mudslides and floods
  • 16 dead in ‘devastating’ Kentucky flooding with death toll expected to double, says state governor
  • Will Smith posts apology video for slapping Chris Rock at Oscars ceremony in March
  • Elon Musk-Twitter trial over US$44 billion deal to start on October 17
  • Ukraine war: US vowed to isolate Russia but Putin’s jet-setting shows it is hard to do
  • Coleen Rooney wins ‘Wagatha Christie’ libel case against Rebekah Vardy
  • Spain prosecutors seek 8-year jail term for singer Shakira over tax fraud
  • Russia says 40 Ukrainian POWs killed in Kyiv missile strike on Donetsk prison
  • Russia tasking mercenaries for front line as infantry losses in Ukraine mount, UK says
  • Joe Biden shrugs off recession talk, declares US ‘on the right path’
  • Pope Francis seeks forgiveness in Canada for sex abuse at residential schools
  • China, slowly but surely, is waking up and smelling the coffee
  • From China to the US, spending on healthcare, housing and education must rise
  • KFC, Pizza Hut operator Yum China reports 54 per cent profit drop after Covid-19 curbs kept diners at bay
  • Global investments in sustainable funds plummet amid fears of looming recession, Morningstar says
  • Exchange Fund, Hong Kong’s currency-peg defender, suffers US$18.4 billion record loss on ‘perfect storm’ of war, disease and slumping markets
  • Threat of ‘heatflation’ looms large as climate change shrinks farm and seafood output, experts say
  • Sandalwood: its history, medicinal uses, Buddhist connections, arrival in China, and how the trade flourished in Asia
  • Get on your bike and ride: top 5 ultra-cycling races for amateurs – from mountains of Kyrgyzstan to wilds of South Africa
  • What is your psychological age? Free online test from Hong Kong-based longevity firm gives answers and tips
  • Ancient tree in Netflix K-drama Extraordinary Attorney Woo draws tourists to a small village in South Korea
  • K-drama midseason recap: Cafe Minamdang – Netflix’s dodgy shaman-themed crime comedy, starring Seo In-guk and Oh Yeon-seo, sidelines its best characters
  • Japanese rail company asks passengers to roll the dice with its tickets to random destinations
  • 4 ways to pick a good wine: leave your usual chardonnay and sauvignon blanc and drink something different
  • Jazz, spiritual and other musical styles blended with Bach to complete Baroque maestro’s Little Organ Book, left unfinished when he died
  • How Hong Kong teachers’ mental health suffered from Covid-19 school closures, which caused 70pc to consider leaving teaching, new study finds
  • Who will get to own the newly discovered Lulo Rose diamond? The 170-carat pink gem is such a rare find, the likes of Louis Vuitton or Graff could snap it up for US$100 million at auction
  • 12 richest NBA players of 2022 – net worths, ranked: from billionaires LeBron James and Michael Jordan and their Nike deals, to Shaquille O’Neal and Magic Johnson – but who became a Coca-Cola CEO?
  • Who was Colonel Tom Parker, Elvis Presley’s manager who allegedly stole over half of his millions? Inside their controversial connection, depicted in Baz Luhrmann’s new biopic
  • Where is Hunter Moore’s ex-girlfriend Kirra Hughes now? The Most Hated Man on the Internet docu showed her side of the story – now she’s modelling, Instagramming and living with a new love
  • Where is Hunter Moore, The Most Hated Man on the Internet, now? Netflix’s latest villain was released from jail in 2017, sold website Is Anyone Up? and has written a book – but he’s still not sorry
  • Why this Taiwan icon is next on our travel bucket list: the avant garde Taipei Performing Arts Centre could join monuments across the globe as a not-to-be-missed architectural attraction
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Guardian: 30 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Uluru statement campaigners welcome Albanese’s referendum commitment
  • Prison attack that killed Ukraine PoWs a war crime, says Zelenskiy, amid calls for UN inquiry
  • At least 16 people dead after flash flooding in Kentucky

  • Rishi Sunak seeks to revive faltering No 10 bid by attacking ‘woke nonsense’

  • Wiegman wants England to ‘show how good we are’ in final against Germany
  • Neighbours finale review – Kylie and Jason make 30 years vanish in an instant

  • Spain reports first death in Europe related to monkeypox outbreak
  • Battle for the Golden Boot: Mead and Popp have dual goals in their sights
  • The paddock that became a grave for the people New Zealand branded ‘defective’ – and chose to forget

  • Will Smith posts emotional apology for the slap: ‘I am deeply remorseful’
  • Boris Johnson is planning to fill the Lords with his cronies and legitimise bribery | Gordon Brown
  • This is the opportunity for England to change the women’s game forever | Karen Carney
  • TikTok refused to host disguised account for Chinese government
  • Making a comeback: rewilding in Europe gets a £4m funding boost
  • Finally, the Wagatha Christie battle is over. But did anyone really win? | Zoe Williams
  • Ireland targets 25% cut in agriculture emissions but farmers voice anger
  • Conversations with James Lovelock, the scientist at the end of the world
  • Loves cheese, hates her first name: 10 things you may not know about Liz Truss
  • ‘Channelling our anger’: Ukrainian Freedom Orchestra heads for the Proms
  • Grubby and dated? Why ‘Wagatha Christie’ may be last libel case of its kind
  • Finland debating stop to tourist visas for Russian citizens
  • Doors shut, ties ditched: what are EU countries doing to save energy?
  • Isaac Chamberlain: ‘There was coke down my leg and I knew I had to get out of that life’
  • So, Prince Harry’s memoir is done – but what’s likely to be in it?
  • Billy Bragg and Tim Martin dine across the divide: ‘Not everyone who voted Brexit is racist, but every racist voted Brexit’
  • Rail workers are Covid heroes – I’m proud to be on the picket line with them | Sam Tarry
  • Mediterranean ecosystem suffering ‘marine wildfire’ as temperatures peak
  • Myths about generous benefits mask the truth of Tory Britain: shamefully low taxes for the rich | Polly Toynbee
  • Wife of executed Myanmar activist says fight for democracy must go on
  • The rouble is soaring and Putin is stronger than ever - our sanctions have backfired | Simon Jenkins
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian
